content.text: What Is a Policy Assignment?

A policy assignment connects a policy definition (or initiative) to a scope. Without an assignment, a policy definition has no effect — it is a rule that exists but is not enforced anywhere. The assignment specifies where the policy applies (scope), what parameter values to use, whether to enforce or just audit, and which sub-scopes to exclude.

The same policy definition can be assigned multiple times at different scopes with different parameters. For example, an allowed-locations policy might be assigned at the Corp MG with a strict region list, and again at an individual sandbox subscription with a broader list — two assignments, one definition, different parameters.

attrs.alt: Policy assignment lifecycle: definition → assign → evaluate → compliance state → remediate

content.text: Creating Assignments via Azure CLI
Basic assignment at management group scope

# Assign a built-in policy at Corp MG with required parameters
az policy assignment create \
  --name 'corp-require-env-tag' \
  --display-name 'Corp: Require environment tag' \
  --policy '1e30110a-5ceb-460c-a204-c1c3969c6d62' \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp' \
  --params '{"tagName":{"value":"environment"}}'

# Verify the assignment was created
az policy assignment show \
  --name 'corp-require-env-tag' \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp' \
  --query '{Name:name,Scope:scope,Policy:policyDefinitionId,Params:parameters}' \
  -o json
Assignment with notScopes (exclusions)

# Assign allowed-locations at Corp MG but exclude the Platform subscriptions
# Platform subs need access to management regions not allowed for workloads
CONNECTIVITY_SUB='/subscriptions/aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa'
IDENTITY_SUB='/subscriptions/bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb'
MANAGEMENT_SUB='/subscriptions/cccccccc-cccc-cccc-cccc-cccccccccccc'

az policy assignment create \
  --name 'corp-allowed-regions' \
  --display-name 'Corp: Allowed regions' \
  --policy 'e56962a6-4747-49cd-b67b-bf8b01975c4f' \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp' \
  --not-scopes "$CONNECTIVITY_SUB $IDENTITY_SUB $MANAGEMENT_SUB" \
  --params '{"listOfAllowedLocations":{"value":["eastus","eastus2","westeurope"]}}'
Assignment with custom non-compliance message

# Custom message helps teams understand WHY their deployment was blocked
az policy assignment create \
  --name 'deny-vm-public-ip' \
  --display-name 'Corp-Prod: Deny VM public IP' \
  --policy '65523614-dabb-4b52-a6ef-5c7e235b3a74' \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp-prod' \
  --non-compliance-message 'message=Public IP on VMs is not allowed in Corp-Prod. Use Azure Bastion for management access or an internal load balancer for application traffic. File a platform ticket if you have an exemption case.'
Enforcement Mode

`Default` enforcement activates deny effects immediately — a deployment that violates the policy is rejected at the ARM layer. `DoNotEnforce` allows all deployments to proceed while still recording compliance state. Use `DoNotEnforce` when introducing a new policy to production environments: you gain visibility into the impact without risking deployment failures for existing teams.

The typical workflow for a new deny policy: assign in DoNotEnforce at the target scope → wait for compliance scan → review non-compliant resources with teams → fix or obtain exemptions → switch enforcement to Default. This prevents surprise deployment failures on day one.

# Assign in DoNotEnforce mode for testing
az policy assignment create \
  --name 'test-deny-public-storage' \
  --policy '4fa4b6c0-31ca-4c0d-b10d-24b96f62a751' \
  --scope '/subscriptions/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111' \
  --enforcement-mode DoNotEnforce

# After reviewing non-compliance, switch to Default enforcement
az policy assignment update \
  --name 'test-deny-public-storage' \
  --scope '/subscriptions/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111' \
  --enforcement-mode Default

# Trigger an immediate compliance scan (default scan cycle is ~24h)
az policy state trigger-scan \
  --subscription 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111
Assignments with Managed Identity (DINE and Modify)

Policies with `deployIfNotExists` or `modify` effects need a managed identity to execute deployments or tag modifications on behalf of the policy. The assignment's managed identity must be granted the role(s) listed in the policy definition's `roleDefinitionIds` array at the assignment scope or broader.

# Assign a deployIfNotExists policy with a system-assigned managed identity
az policy assignment create \
  --name 'deploy-storage-diagnostics' \
  --display-name 'Auto-deploy Storage Diagnostics' \
  --policy 'deploy-storage-diagnostics' \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp' \
  --location eastus \
  --mi-system-assigned \
  --params '{"logAnalyticsWorkspaceId":{"value":"/subscriptions/.../workspaces/central-law"}}'

# Get the managed identity's principal ID
PRINCIPAL=$(az policy assignment show \
  --name 'deploy-storage-diagnostics' \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp' \
  --query 'identity.principalId' -o tsv)

# Grant Contributor at MG scope so the identity can deploy to all Corp subscriptions
az role assignment create \
  --assignee-object-id $PRINCIPAL \
  --role Contributor \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp'

# List all managed identities created by policy assignments
az policy assignment list \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --query '[?identity!=null].{Name:name,Principal:identity.principalId,Type:identity.type}' \
  -o table
Terraform: Assignments at All Scope Levels
Listing and Auditing Assignments

# List all policy assignments at a management group
az policy assignment list \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp' \
  --query '[].{Name:name,DisplayName:displayName,Policy:policyDefinitionId,Enforcement:enforcementMode}' \
  -o table

# List all assignments including inherited ones for a subscription
az policy assignment list \
  --subscription 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 \
  --disable-scope-strict-match \
  --query '[].{Name:displayName,Scope:scope,Effect:parameters.effect.value}' \
  -o table

# Find all assignments referencing a specific policy definition
POLICY_ID='/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/1e30110a-5ceb-460c-a204-c1c3969c6d62'
az policy assignment list \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --query "[?policyDefinitionId=='$POLICY_ID'].{Name:name,Scope:scope}" \
  -o table

# List initiative (policy set) assignments separately
az policy assignment list \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --query "[?policyDefinitionId contains 'policySetDefinitions'].{Name:displayName,Scope:scope}" \
  -o table

# Delete a policy assignment
az policy assignment delete \
  --name 'corp-require-env-tag' \
  --scope '/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp'
Common Assignment Mistakes
Mistake
Consequence
Prevention
Assigning in Default enforcement without testing
Deny blocks existing team deployments on day one; emergency rollback required
Always assign in DoNotEnforce first; switch to Default after reviewing non-compliance
Not setting notScopes for Platform subscriptions
Allowed-locations deny blocks platform networking resources in non-standard regions
Exclude Platform (Connectivity, Identity, Management) subscriptions from location-restrict policies
Assigning DINE policy without granting identity the required role
Policy appears assigned; DINE never executes; no error shown on the assignment
Immediately after assignment, grant the identity's principal the role from roleDefinitionIds at the assignment scope
Not setting nonComplianceMessages on deny assignments
Teams get a generic Azure error when their deployment is blocked; platform team gets support tickets
Always add a custom message with the reason and the path to request an exemption
Assigning the same policy at both MG and subscription scope
Duplicate evaluations; assignment count grows unnecessarily; harder to audit effective policy set
Assign at the broadest scope that applies; use notScopes for exceptions rather than duplicate assignments
Terraform: Policy Assignments Across Scope Levels

Terraform's azurerm provider exposes azurerm_resource_policy_assignment, azurerm_resource_group_policy_assignment, azurerm_subscription_policy_assignment, and azurerm_management_group_policy_assignment as four distinct resources — one per scope level. This lets you codify the full assignment model and catch scope errors at plan time rather than deployment time.

Terraform's depends_on is required when the role assignment references the managed identity's principal_id from a policy assignment — the principal is only created after the assignment is applied, so the role assignment cannot be planned in the same apply without the dependency.
Parameter Value Strategies

Policy assignments often need to supply parameter values that differ between environments or scopes. The following patterns handle the three common scenarios: per-environment values, values derived from the subscription context, and values managed centrally in a parameter store.

# ── Pattern 1: Per-Environment Parameter File ──────────────────────────
# Maintain separate parameter JSON files for dev/prod assignments

# prod-allowed-locations-params.json
cat <<'EOF' > prod-allowed-locations-params.json
{
  "listOfAllowedLocations": {
    "value": ["eastus2", "westus2", "northeurope"]
  }
}
EOF

az policy assignment create \
  --name allowed-locations-prod \
  --subscription $PROD_SUB_ID \
  --policy /providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/e56962a6-4747-49cd-b67b-bf8b01975c4f \
  --params @prod-allowed-locations-params.json

# ── Pattern 2: Dynamic Parameter Value from ARM Function ───────────────
# Some parameters can reference subscription metadata via ARM template functions
# This is not supported in policy assignment params directly,
# but you can resolve subscription display name via az and pass it in:
SUB_NAME=$(az account show --query 'name' -o tsv)
az policy assignment create \
  --name tag-sub-name \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --policy $CUSTOM_DEF_ID \
  --params "{\"environmentName\":{\"value\":\"${SUB_NAME}\"}}"

# ── Pattern 3: Initiative Parameter Override at Assignment ─────────────
# An initiative may expose composite parameters — override specific nested params
az policy assignment create \
  --name corp-security-baseline \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --policy-set-definition /providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policySetDefinitions/1f3afdf9-d0c9-4c3d-847f-89da613e70a8 \
  --params '{
    "logAnalyticsWorkspaceId": {"value": "/subscriptions/11111111.../resourceGroups/rg-platform/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/law-corp"},
    "effect": {"value": "DeployIfNotExists"}
  }'
Auditing and Monitoring Policy Assignments

With hundreds of assignments possible across a management group hierarchy, visibility into the current assignment state is critical. These queries and commands provide a point-in-time snapshot and detect assignment drift from the expected baseline.

# List all assignments in scope (MG, subscription, RG, resource)
# Management group and all children:
az policy assignment list \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --query '[].{Name:name,Display:displayName,Scope:scope,Enforce:enforcementMode}' \
  -o table

# Assignments targeting a specific subscription (excludes inherited):
az policy assignment list \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --query '[?scope==\`/subscriptions/$SUB_ID\`].{Name:name,Display:displayName,Effect:policyDefinitionId}' \
  -o table

# Show full details of a specific assignment (parameters, notScopes, identity):
az policy assignment show \
  --name required-tag-cost-center \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID \
  -o json | jq '{displayName,enforcementMode,parameters,notScopes,identity}'

# Find all assignments for a specific policy definition across all subscriptions:
az account list --query '[].id' -o tsv | while read SUB; do
  az policy assignment list --subscription $SUB \
    --query "[?policyDefinitionId contains 'require-cost-center'].{Sub:'$SUB',Name:name}" \
    -o tsv 2>/dev/null
done

// Resource Graph — policy assignments with compliance state
// Shows each assignment's non-compliant resource count
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.PolicyInsights/PolicyStates'
| where properties.complianceState == 'NonCompliant'
| summarize NonCompliantCount = count() by
    policyAssignmentId = tostring(properties.policyAssignmentId),
    policyAssignmentName = tostring(properties.policyAssignmentName),
    policyDefinitionAction = tostring(properties.policyDefinitionAction)
| order by NonCompliantCount desc
| project policyAssignmentName, policyDefinitionAction, NonCompliantCount

// Find assignments with DoNotEnforce on subscriptions that should be enforcing
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments'
| where properties.enforcementMode == 'DoNotEnforce'
| project
    name,
    displayName = tostring(properties.displayName),
    scope = tostring(properties.scope),
    enforcementMode = tostring(properties.enforcementMode)
| where scope has '/subscriptions/'
| order by scope asc

// Count assignments by scope level
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments'
| extend scope = tostring(properties.scope)
| extend scopeLevel = case(
    scope has '/resourceGroups/' and scope has '/providers/', 'Resource',
    scope has '/resourceGroups/', 'ResourceGroup',
    scope has '/subscriptions/', 'Subscription',
    'ManagementGroup')
| summarize AssignmentCount = count() by scopeLevel
| order by AssignmentCount desc
Common Assignment Mistakes and Fixes
Mistake
Symptom
Fix
Assigning deny policy without testing in DoNotEnforce first
Legitimate deployments blocked unexpectedly; on-call escalations
Use DoNotEnforce for 7+ days, review compliance report, then switch to Deny
Missing managed identity on DINE/Modify assignment
Policy evaluates but remediation task fails with 403
Re-create assignment with --mi-system-assigned and grant the required RBAC role
Using notScopes to exclude a child subscription when assigning at MG
Resources in excluded subscription not evaluated — no compliance data
notScopes must use full ARM scope path; verify with az policy state list
Assigning an initiative before customizing parameter defaults
Initiative deploys with placeholder parameter values; agents point to wrong workspace
Create assignment with explicit --params file; never rely on built-in defaults for workspace IDs
Updating policy definition without re-evaluating existing assignments
Assignment still shows old compliance state for up to 24h
Run az policy state trigger-scan after any definition change
Overlapping deny assignments at different scopes with conflicting notScopes
Resources compliant at RG scope but non-compliant at subscription scope
Use az policy state list --filter to see which assignment is triggering the deny
Assignment Remediation Tasks

When a DeployIfNotExists or Modify policy assignment is created, it only evaluates new or updated resources going forward. Existing non-compliant resources require a remediation task to trigger the policy effect retroactively. Remediation tasks run asynchronously and can be scoped to a subset of resources.

# Create a remediation task for an existing DINE assignment
az policy remediation create \
  --name remediate-law-agent \
  --policy-assignment /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/deploy-law-agent \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --resource-discovery-mode ReEvaluateCompliance

# Check remediation task status
az policy remediation show \
  --name remediate-law-agent \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --query '{Status:provisioningState,SuccessCount:deploymentSummary.successfulDeployments,FailCount:deploymentSummary.failedDeployments}'

# List all remediation tasks and their status
az policy remediation list \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --query '[].{Name:name,Status:provisioningState,Total:deploymentSummary.totalDeployments,Success:deploymentSummary.successfulDeployments}' \
  -o table

# Cancel a running remediation task (if it's remediating too many resources at once)
az policy remediation cancel \
  --name remediate-law-agent \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Scope a remediation task to a single resource group (partial remediation)
az policy remediation create \
  --name remediate-law-agent-rg-api \
  --policy-assignment /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/deploy-law-agent \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --resource-group rg-api \
  --resource-discovery-mode ReEvaluateCompliance

Remediation tasks use the policy assignment's managed identity to perform the deployments. If the managed identity lacks Contributor (or the specific role in roleDefinitionIds) on the remediation scope, every deployment in the task will fail with a 403 error. Always verify identity role assignments before running a large-scale remediation.
Summary

A policy assignment activates a policy definition at a specific scope. The assignment specifies the scope (MG, subscription, or resource group), parameter overrides, enforcement mode (Default blocks; DoNotEnforce audits only), sub-scope exclusions via notScopes, and a managed identity for DINE/Modify effects. The lifecycle follows: assign in DoNotEnforce → review non-compliance → fix or exempt → switch to Default enforcement. Assignments should be managed as code in Terraform or Bicep to maintain auditability, prevent drift, and support the consistent governance model that management group hierarchy makes possible.
